description:
Trigger: an async application calls Pkcs11Backend::from_uri(...) directly from inside a tokio current_thread runtime (or any single-threaded async executor), e.g. during application startup before the runtime has many tasks to swap in.

Bad behavior: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:62-127 performs synchronous I/O — Pkcs11::new() calls dlopen on the PKCS#11 .so (filesystem read, possibly nfs), ctx.initialize() calls C_Initialize on the library which for network-backed HSMs (CloudHSM, gcp-kms-pkcs11) opens TCP connections, then get_slots_with_token() makes another HSM round-trip. On a network HSM these can take 100ms-multi-second. All of this happens on the calling thread without a spawn_blocking. Inside a tokio runtime this stalls the executor; in a current_thread runtime, every other task on that thread is paused until init returns.

The lib doc on from_uri (line 60-61) says 'No session is opened until a trait method is called' which is misleading — initialize() and get_slots_with_token() ARE session-adjacent operations and DO open library state, including TCP for network HSMs.

Fix: either (a) document loudly that from_uri must not be called from async context, recommending tokio::task::spawn_blocking around it; or (b) make from_uri lazy like wolfhsm does (secretx-wolfhsm validates URI/env at construction, opens connection on first use); option (b) is the consistent pattern across the workspace.
title: from_uri blocks the calling thread on PKCS#11 dlopen + C_Initialize + slot enumeration
